description: Fraudsters send UPI collect requests posing as refunds or cashback. Victim approves thinking they are receiving money but actually sends money from their account. Common on PhonePe, GPay, and Paytm.
recommended_action: Never approve unsolicited UPI collect requests. Verify sender identity through official channels. Only accept collect requests from known contacts.

description: SMS or WhatsApp messages claiming bank KYC has expired with a link to a fake banking portal that steals login credentials and OTP. Targets HDFC, SBI, ICICI, Axis customers.
recommended_action: Banks never ask for KYC via SMS links. Visit official bank website or branch directly. Report phishing SMS to your bank.

description: Scammers impersonate CBI, police, or RBI officers. They claim victim's account is linked to money laundering or drug trafficking. Threaten immediate arrest. Demand transfer to safe government account. Often target elderly people.
recommended_action: Government agencies never call demanding money transfers. Hang up immediately. Real CBI/police serve notices in person. Call 100 to verify any police-related call.

description: Scammers place their own QR codes over legitimate merchant QR codes at restaurants, petrol pumps, and parking lots. Payments go to scammer's account instead of the merchant.
recommended_action: Always verify the payee name displayed on your UPI app before confirming payment. If the name doesn't match the merchant, do not proceed.

pattern_name: Fake Job Offer Phishing via WhatsApp
description: Fake job offers from major companies sent via WhatsApp. Links lead to professional-looking application forms that harvest Aadhaar, PAN, and bank account details. Sometimes require upfront registration fee.
recommended_action: Legitimate companies never ask for sensitive documents or fees during initial application. Apply only through official company career pages. Report to cybercrime.gov.in.

description: Fraudsters convince telecom provider to issue duplicate SIM card. Once they control the phone number, they intercept OTPs to access bank accounts and make unauthorized transactions.
recommended_action: Set a SIM lock PIN. If your phone suddenly loses network, contact your telecom provider immediately. Enable app-based 2FA instead of SMS-based where possible.

description: SMS claims electricity bill is overdue and service will be disconnected within 24 hours. Contains a link to a fake payment portal that captures bank details. Targets state electricity board customers.
recommended_action: Electricity boards never send disconnection threats via SMS with payment links. Check bills through official app or website only.

description: Callers impersonate FedEx, DHL, or India Post claiming a parcel with illegal items has been intercepted. Transfer call to fake police officer who demands payment to avoid arrest. Uses spoofed numbers and IVR.
recommended_action: Courier companies never claim illegal items by phone. Hang up and call the courier company directly using the number from their official website.

description: Scammers call claiming to be Microsoft or antivirus support. Trick victims into installing remote access tools like TeamViewer or AnyDesk. Once connected, steal banking credentials or install malware.
recommended_action: Microsoft never makes unsolicited tech support calls. Never install remote access software at a stranger's request. If installed, uninstall immediately and scan for malware.

description: Fraudulent investment schemes on Instagram, Telegram, and YouTube promising 30-50% monthly returns on crypto or forex trading. Often uses fake celebrity endorsements and testimonials.
recommended_action: Returns above 15-20% annually are almost certainly fraudulent. Only invest through SEBI-registered brokers. Report to Economic Offences Wing.

description: Physical skimming devices placed over ATM card reader slots with hidden cameras to capture PINs. Cloned cards used for unauthorized withdrawals. Often found at standalone ATMs.
recommended_action: Check for loose card slots before inserting card. Cover keypad while entering PIN. Prefer ATMs inside bank branches. Enable SMS alerts for all transactions.

description: Individuals posing as food delivery agents (Swiggy, Zomato) arrive at doorstep claiming system error and requesting extra payment via personal QR code. Sometimes also steal package contents.
recommended_action: Delivery agents never ask for extra payment. Always verify order status in the official app. Report to the platform's customer care.

description: Emails impersonating Income Tax Department asking to verify Aadhaar or PAN for tax refund processing. Links to fake e-filing portal that captures credentials and Aadhaar details.
recommended_action: IT Department sends notices only through the official e-filing portal. Never click links in emails about tax refunds. Login directly at incometaxindiaefiling.gov.in.

description: Attacker contacts victim pretending to be a friend or family member saying they accidentally sent their WhatsApp OTP to victim's number and requests it back. Used to take over victim's WhatsApp account.
recommended_action: Never share any OTP with anyone, even if they claim to be family. Enable WhatsApp two-step verification. If compromised, re-register WhatsApp immediately.

pattern_name: Lottery and Prize Draw Scam via WhatsApp Video Call
description: Scammers initiate WhatsApp video calls showing a pre-recorded live draw where victim's number wins. Request processing fees via UPI. Video is pre-recorded and loops.
recommended_action: You cannot win a lottery you never entered. Never pay processing fees for prizes. Block and report the number.

description: Ransomware attacks targeting small and medium businesses through phishing emails with infected attachments. Encrypts business data and demands Bitcoin ransom. Often exploits unpatched systems.
recommended_action: Maintain regular offline backups. Keep all software updated. Train employees to recognize phishing emails. Report to CERT-In at incident@cert-in.org.in.

description: Fake Aadhaar update centers collecting biometric data and charging inflated fees. May use harvested biometrics for identity theft and fraudulent authentication.
recommended_action: Only use official Aadhaar enrollment centers listed on uidai.gov.in. Official update fee is Rs 50. Lock your Aadhaar biometrics at myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in when not in use.

description: Motorcycle-borne assailants targeting pedestrians, especially women, during early morning or late evening hours. Common in residential areas during low-traffic times.
recommended_action: Avoid wearing expensive jewelry during walks. Stay alert and walk facing traffic. Use well-lit main roads. Report to nearest police station immediately.

description: Data breaches at health tech startups and hospitals exposing patient records including Aadhaar, medical histories, and insurance details. Often due to misconfigured cloud databases.
recommended_action: Change passwords for any health platform accounts. Monitor bank statements for 30 days. If Aadhaar is exposed, lock biometrics at myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in.

description: Data leaks from telecom providers exposing subscriber details including phone numbers, email addresses, and location data. Data sold on dark web forums.
recommended_action: Change telecom account password. Enable 2FA on telecom account. Do not reuse passwords across services. Monitor for suspicious account activity.

pattern_name: Fake Parking Fee QR Code Scam
description: Scammers in fake municipal uniforms collect parking fees via personal QR codes at malls, markets, and metro stations. Printed rate cards make it look official.
recommended_action: Use only official parking apps or authorized POS machines. Verify payee name before scanning any QR code. Report fake attendants to local municipal authority.

description: Scammers trick victims into installing screen sharing apps to see OTPs and banking notifications in real time. Often combined with customer care impersonation.
recommended_action: Never install screen sharing apps at anyone's request. No legitimate company will ask to see your screen. If installed unknowingly, uninstall immediately and change all banking passwords.

description: Organized gangs targeting parked two-wheelers and cars during late night hours (2-5 AM). Use loading tempos to quickly move stolen vehicles. Common near railway stations and markets.
recommended_action: Use both disc lock and chain lock. Park in well-lit areas with CCTV coverage. Install GPS tracker if possible. Register vehicle on Vahan portal for easier recovery.

description: Attackers exploit default router credentials to gain network access. Set up DNS redirection to intercept traffic or inject ads. Common on older routers with default admin passwords.
recommended_action: Change default router admin password immediately. Use WPA3 encryption. Disable WPS. Regularly check connected devices list. Update router firmware.

pattern_name: Fake Customer Care Number on Google
description: Scammers create fake customer care numbers for banks and companies that appear in Google search results. Victims calling these numbers are tricked into sharing account details.
recommended_action: Never search for customer care numbers on Google. Use only numbers from official bank apps, websites, or the back of your debit/credit card.
helpline: Your bank's official app or card-back number
